metrics.name: visionOS
metrics.question: Will Apple announce visionOS 27 (or a successor major version to visionOS 26) at WWDC 2026?

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

metrics.mechanism_failure_proof: the resolution criterion carries no parseable numeric threshold — a bounded numeric mechanism is not the right bridge for this terminal

simulation_result.question: Will Apple announce visionOS 27 (or a successor major version to visionOS 26) at WWDC 2026?

structural_disagreement.treatment: kept separated by model; the more-resolved model's conditional is the simulation input, the spread widens the interval

limitations: forecast decomposition — grounded prior 0.8333 (counted n=2); simulation-conditional 1.0 (resolved mass 0.75); headline 0.9583 via combiner_unavailable_range_only. Prior and simulation are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule.
bounded omitted-state residual 0.278: the interval widens to [0.722, 1.0] — private-state omissions are BOUNDS, never unknown-state worlds
unresolved [unresolved_missing_mechanism]: 0.250 — one repair attempt else under-modeled disclosure

no leakage-audited prior↔simulation reliability combiner is fitted — the headline is the mass-based recovery blend (resolved mass keeps its simulated conditional; unresolved mass takes the grounded prior); the two inputs are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule

blueprint.causal_thesis: Apple's executive leadership, particularly Tim Cook and the software engineering team, must decide whether to allocate resources to a major visionOS release given the platform's abandonment reports. The decision hinges on whether Apple views WWDC 2026 as an opportunity to signal continued commitment

pruned.reason: no action, trigger, target or observation channel reaches a surviving terminal pathway (evidence-mention-only / non-causal)

apple_os_naming_convention.claim: Apple transitioned to year-based OS naming starting with WWDC 2025, so the third major release of visionOS was called 'visionOS 26' rather than 'visionOS 3'.

provenance.definition: Apple transitioned to year-based OS naming starting with WWDC 2025, so the third major release of visionOS was called 'visionOS 26' rather than 'visionOS 3'.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At WWDC 2025, Apple announced visionOS 26 as the successor to visionOS 2, adopting year-based naming for all major OS releases.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro, introducing new spatial computing features.

vision_pro_platform_abandonment_reports.claim: Reports indicate Apple has effectively abandoned the Vision Pro platform, with stopped work on new models and team redistribution.

provenance.definition: Reports indicate Apple has effectively abandoned the Vision Pro platform, with stopped work on new models and team redistribution.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: MacRumors reported on April 29, 2026 that Apple has stopped work on Vision Pro, redistributed the team, and cancelled the Vision Air project.
cases.basis_quote: Apple has reportedly stopped work on Vision Pro, redistributed the team to other projects, cancelled the 'Vision Air' project, and shifted focus toward AI-powered smart glasses.

wwdc_annual_os_announcement_pattern.claim: Apple has historically announced major new versions of its operating systems at WWDC each June.

provenance.definition: Apple has historically announced major new versions of its operating systems at WWDC each June.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At WWDC 2024, Apple announced visionOS 2 as the first major update to visionOS.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 2, the first major update to the operating system for Apple Vision Pro, bringing new spatial computing capabilities.

cases.description: At WWDC 2025, Apple announced visionOS 26, continuing the annual major OS update cycle.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: Apple announces a new major version of visionOS at WWDC 2026

provenance.definition: A public announcement by Apple, either during the WWDC 2026 keynote or via an official Apple Newsroom press release, of a new major visionOS version intended to succeed visionOS 26.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any mention of a new major visionOS version (e.g., visionOS 27, visionOS 3) during the WWDC 2026 keynote or in an Apple Newsroom press release between May 6, 2026 and June 12, 2026.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Minor point updates (e.g., visionOS 26.5, 26.6) or announcements after June 12, 2026.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At WWDC 2025, Apple announced visionOS 26 via keynote and Newsroom press release.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.

cases.description: At WWDC 2024, Apple announced visionOS 2 via keynote and Newsroom press release.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 2, the first major update to the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.

apple_wwdc_keynote.consequence_of_nonparticipation: No announcement of visionOS version at WWDC keynote

apple_newsroom.consequence_of_nonparticipation: No Newsroom announcement of visionOS version

weight_invariant.rule: every weight traces to a counted reference class or is explicit unknown mass; no qualitative label is mapped to a number

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

tim_cook.residual_provenance: uncovered feasible option(s) ['include_in_keynote'] with no counted cases — bounded per option

marketing_team.residual_provenance: uncovered feasible option(s) ['publish_newsroom'] with no counted cases — bounded per option

tim_cook.claim: Tim Cook privately believes the Vision Pro platform remains strategically important for Apple's long-term innovation narrative, despite weak sales. He sees a major visionOS update as necessary to signal commitment to developers and early adopters, and to maintain the platform's viability until futur
beliefs: visionOS is a foundational OS for spatial computing
abandoning now would damage Apple's reputation for long-term investment
goals: preserve optionality for future AR/glasses products
commitments: publicly stated Apple is 'all in' on spatial computing at 2024 shareholder meeting
tim_cook.pressures: Board concerns about R&D ROI; internal factions pushing to cut losses

relationships.marketing_team: delegates messaging but expects alignment with strategic narrative

historical_case_refs: Apple continued macOS development during PowerPC-to-Intel transition despite low sales
distinguishing_observations: Would personally approve keynote inclusion of visionOS 27
tim_cook.action_if_state: Direct marketing_team to include a visionOS 27 segment in the WWDC keynote, emphasizing new features and developer tools.
transition_triggers: New internal sales data showing uptick

tim_cook.claim: Tim Cook privately accepts that Vision Pro is a failed product and that further investment in visionOS is wasteful. He prioritizes reallocating engineering resources to AI and services, and views a major OS announcement as misleading to investors and developers. He prefers a quiet maintenance mode o
beliefs: Vision Pro is a niche dead-end
Apple must focus on AI to compete
commitments: internal memo (leaked) stating 'we must be ruthless with underperforming projects'
tim_cook.pressures: Analyst calls to cut losses; strong performance of Services division

relationships.marketing_team: instructed to downplay visionOS in favor of iOS/macOS AI features

historical_case_refs: Apple quietly discontinued the Xserve without a major OS update
distinguishing_observations: Would veto any mention of visionOS 27 in keynote or press release
tim_cook.action_if_state: Instruct marketing_team to omit visionOS 27 from WWDC keynote and issue no visionOS press release; instead, announce only minor visionOS 26.x updates.
assumptions: No contractual obligation to developers for a major update

marketing_team.claim: The marketing team has prepared a polished segment for the WWDC keynote announcing visionOS 27, including new features (e.g., improved spatial Persona, enterprise APIs) and a developer beta. They believe this aligns with Apple's tradition of annual OS updates and counters abandonment rumors.
beliefs: WWDC keynote must include all major OS updates
visionOS 27 is necessary to retain developer trust
commitments: internal schedule shows visionOS 27 segment rehearsed
marketing_team.pressures: Short keynote time; competition from iOS 27 and macOS 27 demos

relationships.tim_cook: seeks final approval but expects inclusion

historical_case_refs: WWDC 2025 included visionOS 26 despite low sales
marketing_team.action_if_state: Present visionOS 27 segment during WWDC keynote as planned, with demo and beta availability.
assumptions: Tim Cook does not veto at last minute

marketing_team.claim: The marketing team has been directed to remove any visionOS 27 content from the keynote and instead issue only a minor point update (visionOS 26.6) via press release. They believe this is to avoid highlighting a failing platform and to focus messaging on AI and services.
beliefs: visionOS is a liability for the keynote narrative
minor update suffices for existing users
goals: avoid negative attention on Vision Pro
commitments: final keynote script reviewed without visionOS 27
marketing_team.pressures: Strong directive from Tim Cook; limited time for other demos
historical_case_refs: WWDC 2023 did not announce a major watchOS update (only watchOS 9.x) when Apple Watch sales slumped
distinguishing_observations: No visionOS 27 slide in final keynote deck
marketing_team.action_if_state: Omit visionOS 27 from keynote; publish a brief press release on June 8 announcing visionOS 26.6 with bug fixes.
assumptions: No last-minute change from Tim Cook

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: I don't have fresh data on developer sentiment or board member concerns since the last review.
deliberations.note: I have no new data on developer sentiment or board concerns, but my existing knowledge tells me that the developer ecosystem is fragile and that board members are anxious about R&D spend. Including vi

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether Tim expects a major announcement or just a brief update.
deliberations.note: Tim's directive was a keynote segment, which implies a public-facing moment. A Newsroom press release alone might be seen as sidestepping his intent, risking his displeasure. However, a major announce

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: We don't know exactly how much time Tim will allocate to visionOS in the keynote, or whether he has already decided to prioritize other
deliberations.note: Publishing the Newsroom press release now risks diluting the keynote's impact and could frustrate Tim if he planned a different narrative flow. However, delaying it might signal uncertainty to develop

include_in_keynote.description: Tim Cook approves inclusion of visionOS major version in keynote.
authority_required: Final approval on major product announcements

publish_newsroom.description: Marketing team publishes a Newsroom press release announcing major visionOS version.

novel_cast_vote_silent_phase_out.description: Tim Cook casts an internal vote to initiate silent phase-out of Vision Pro, reallocate engineering resources to AI features in iOS/macOS, and signal to analysts that Apple is prioritizing high-margin Services and AI.
